CheckTitle: Conditional Access policy covers all cloud apps and all users
Description: Helper check that verifies whether Microsoft Entra Conditional Access includes at least one enabled policy scoped to all cloud applications and all users. Policies that only require a password change and report-only policies are excluded from passing. The result is intended to support other, more specific Conditional Access checks rather than enforce a baseline on its own.
Risk: Without a Conditional Access policy that targets all cloud apps and all users, newly added applications or user accounts may not be covered by existing access controls until policies are explicitly updated.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center at https://entra.microsoft.com.
2. Expand **Protection** > **Conditional Access** and select **Policies**.
3. Click **New policy**.
4. Under **Users**, select **Include** > **All users**. Exclude break-glass accounts as needed.
5. Under **Target resources**, select **Include** > **All cloud apps**.
6. Under **Grant**, select the desired access controls.
7. Set the policy to **On** and click **Create**.

Recommendation.Text: Ensure there is at least one enabled Conditional Access policy that targets all cloud apps and all users so that other Conditional Access checks have broad coverage to evaluate. Exclude only break-glass accounts.

Notes: Generic helper check intended to support other Conditional Access checks. Conditional Access policies require Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 licenses.
